questions.question: Without colour to rely on, black and white images depend most on…
questions.reviewHint: B&W relies on tonal contrast, shapes, lines and texture rather than colour.

questions.question: Why is shooting in RAW recommended for black and white?
options: It keeps full colour data, giving more control over the B&W conversion
It bakes in the conversion permanently
It removes the need for editing
questions.reviewHint: RAW retains all data so you can control the conversion and tones in editing.

questions.question: If you set your camera's monochrome (B&W) mode while shooting RAW…
options: The RAW still keeps colour data; only the preview/JPEG looks B&W
questions.reviewHint: Monochrome mode previews in B&W but the RAW retains colour for flexible conversion.

questions.question: Strong black and white images often emphasise…
questions.reviewHint: Shape, line and texture carry a B&W image in the absence of colour.

questions.question: Contrast between light and shadow is important in B&W because it…
options: Creates depth, drama and separation between elements
questions.reviewHint: Light-to-dark contrast gives B&W images depth and impact.

questions.question: When converting a colour image to black and white, channel/colour sliders let you…
options: Control how each original colour translates into a grey tone
questions.reviewHint: B&W conversion tools map each colour to a brightness, letting you tune the tones.

questions.question: A scene may suit black and white when…
options: Colour is distracting and tone/texture/form carry the image
The colours are the main point
It is a product shot needing accurate colour
questions.reviewHint: B&W shines when colour distracts and the strength lies in form, tone and texture.

questions.question: Leading lines and composition rules in B&W are…
options: Just as important as in colour - arguably more so
questions.reviewHint: Composition matters even more in B&W since colour can't do the heavy lifting.

questions.question: Adjusting tones and contrast in post for B&W is used to…
options: Shape the mood and emphasise the subject
questions.reviewHint: Tonal and contrast adjustments set the mood and direct attention in B&W.

questions.question: A benefit of black and white photography is that it…
options: Can create a timeless, emotive feel by removing colour distractions
questions.reviewHint: Stripping colour can lend a timeless, emotive quality and focus attention on form.
